edges.description: Loss of IL-7/JAK-STAT signaling halts thymocyte survival and proliferation, blocking maturation of T lymphocytes.
Failure of antigen-receptor gene recombination arrests thymocyte maturation, blocking T-cell development.
edges.source: Defective V(D)J recombination and DNA repair

edges.description: Lymphotoxic deoxyadenosine/dATP accumulation impairs thymocyte survival and blocks T-cell development.
intermediate_mechanisms: deoxyadenosine and intracellular dATP accumulation; lymphocyte apoptosis

edges.description: Absence of functional T lymphocytes abolishes cell-mediated immunity and (directly or via loss of T-cell help) humoral immunity.
Blocked thymic T-cell development produces profound T lymphocytopenia.
Absence of developing thymocytes leaves a small, dysplastic thymus that yields an absent thymic shadow on imaging.
In T-B- subtypes (RAG1/RAG2, Artemis, ADA) the same developmental/metabolic block also abolishes B-lymphocyte development.
intermediate_mechanisms: shared V(D)J recombination or metabolic block affecting the B-lymphoid lineage

edges.description: In NK-negative subtypes (IL2RG, JAK3, ADA) the gamma-chain signaling or metabolic defect also abolishes NK-cell development.
intermediate_mechanisms: shared common gamma-chain signaling or metabolic defect affecting the NK lineage
edges.target: Reduced total natural killer cell count

edges.description: Loss of adaptive immunity is the defining clinical immunodeficiency.
Loss of functional T cells abolishes cell-mediated immune responses.
Without adaptive immunity, infants suffer recurrent and persistent infections from early infancy.
Loss of T-cell immunity predisposes to fungal infection including persistent candidiasis.
Defective cellular immunity allows persistent oral Candida infection.
Loss of adaptive immunity predisposes to recurrent and opportunistic pneumonia (including Pneumocystis).
Opportunistic enteric infection and immune dysfunction produce persistent diarrhea.
intermediate_mechanisms: opportunistic enteric and viral gastrointestinal infection

edges.description: Chronic infection and diarrhea cause poor linear growth and weight gain.
intermediate_mechanisms: chronic infection and persistent diarrhea with poor weight gain

edges.description: Disseminated infection and immune dysregulation (e.g., Omenn syndrome) can produce hepatosplenomegaly.

edges.target: Defective V(D)J recombination and DNA repair

evidence.snippet: Adenosine deaminase (ADA) deficiency is a systemic purine metabolic disorder that primarily affects lymphocyte development, viability, and function.

nodes.description: Without functional T cells (and often B cells), the patient cannot mount protective cellular or humoral adaptive immune responses, leaving the infant susceptible to overwhelming bacterial, viral, fungal, and protozoal infection.

evidence.snippet: Characteristic immune abnormalities are lymphocytopenia (low numbers of T, B, and NK cells) combined with the absence of both humoral and cellular immune function.

nodes.description: Cell-mediated immune responses are absent owing to loss of functional T lymphocytes.

evidence.snippet: Characteristic immune abnormalities are lymphocytopenia (low numbers of T, B, and NK cells) combined with the absence of both humoral and cellular immune function.

nodes.description: A small, dysplastic thymus produces the characteristic absent thymic shadow on chest imaging.

evidence.reference_title: The diagnosis of severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID): The Primary Immune Deficiency Treatment Consortium (PIDTC) 2022 Definitions.
evidence.snippet: Severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID) results from defects in the differentiation of hematopoietic stem cells into mature T lymphocytes

nodes.description: The convergent consequence of all SCID subtypes is failure of hematopoietic stem cells to differentiate into mature, functional T lymphocytes in the thymus, yielding absent or severely reduced autologous T cells. Depending on the genetic lesion, B and/or NK lineages are also affected.

evidence.reference_title: The diagnosis of severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID): The Primary Immune Deficiency Treatment Consortium (PIDTC) 2022 Definitions.
evidence.snippet: Severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID) results from defects in the differentiation of hematopoietic stem cells into mature T lymphocytes, with additional lymphoid lineages affected in particular genotypes.

nodes.description: Persistent diarrhea, often from opportunistic enteric infection, is a common presenting feature.

evidence.snippet: poor linear growth and weight gain secondary to persistent diarrhea

nodes.description: Persistent oral thrush is a frequent early manifestation of defective cellular immunity.

evidence.snippet: Clinical manifestations are characterised by recurrent and severe bacterial, viral, and fungal opportunistic infections that start from early infancy period.

nodes.description: In X-linked SCID, JAK3 deficiency, and IL7R deficiency, loss-of-function in the common gamma chain (IL2RG), its associated kinase JAK3, or the IL-7 receptor alpha chain abolishes IL-7-dependent JAK-STAT signaling that drives early thymocyte survival and proliferation, blocking T-cell development. IL2RG and JAK3 defects additionally abrogate NK-cell development (T-B+NK-), whereas IL7R defects spare B and NK cells (T-B+NK+).

evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of typical and atypical X-SCID is established in a male proband with suggestive findings and a hemizygous pathogenic variant in IL2RG identified by molecular genetic testing.

nodes.description: B lymphocytes are absent in T-B- forms (RAG1/RAG2, ADA, Artemis) and preserved in T-B+ forms.

evidence.snippet: Characteristic immune abnormalities are lymphocytopenia (low numbers of T, B, and NK cells)

nodes.description: Profound T lymphocytopenia (<0.05 x 10^9 autologous T cells/L in typical SCID).

evidence.reference_title: The diagnosis of severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID): The Primary Immune Deficiency Treatment Consortium (PIDTC) 2022 Definitions.
evidence.snippet: Patients with typical SCID must have less than 0.05 × 109 autologous T cells/L on repetitive testing

nodes.description: In RAG1, RAG2, and DCLRE1C (Artemis) deficiency, the machinery that assembles antigen-receptor genes is impaired. RAG1/RAG2 initiate site-specific DNA cleavage during V(D)J recombination, and Artemis opens the resulting DNA hairpins as part of non-homologous end-joining. Failure to recombine T-cell (and B-cell) receptor genes arrests lymphocyte development (T-B-NK+); Artemis loss additionally confers cellular radiosensitivity.
nodes.id: Defective V(D)J recombination and DNA repair

evidence.snippet: Severe Combined Immunodeficiency (SCID) is an inherited group of rare, life-threatening disorders due to the defect in T cell development and function.

nodes.description: For ADA deficiency, enzyme replacement therapy with PEGylated adenosine deaminase corrects the metabolic defect, providing protection against infection; it is often used to stabilize patients before curative HSCT or gene therapy.

evidence.snippet: enzyme replacement therapy (ERT) by intramuscular administration of PEGylated ADA

nodes.description: Poor linear growth and weight gain secondary to chronic infection and persistent diarrhea.

evidence.snippet: Untreated ADA-SCID presents as life-threatening opportunistic illnesses in the first weeks to months of life with poor linear growth and weight gain secondary to persistent diarrhea

nodes.description: Autologous hematopoietic stem cell gene therapy corrects the causative gene in the patient's own stem cells; it is an established alternative to allogeneic HSCT for ADA-SCID (Strimvelis) and has shown clinical success for X-linked and other SCID forms.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term and real-world safety and efficacy of retroviral gene therapy for adenosine deaminase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Long-term persistence of multilineage gene-corrected cells, metabolic detoxification, immune reconstitution and decreased infection rates were observed.

nodes.description: Allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation is the definitive curative treatment for SCID, reconstituting a functional immune system; outcomes are best when performed early, before the onset of infections.

evidence.snippet: Haematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) is the treatment of choice.

nodes.description: Hepatosplenomegaly occurs particularly in Omenn syndrome and with disseminated infection.

evidence.reference_title: The diagnosis of severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID): The Primary Immune Deficiency Treatment Consortium (PIDTC) 2022 Definitions.
evidence.snippet: Omenn syndrome requires a generalized erythematous rash, absent transplacentally acquired maternal engraftment, and 2 or more of these: eosinophilia, elevated IgE, lymphadenopathy, hepatosplenomegaly.
The diagnosis of typical and atypical X-SCID is established in a male proband with suggestive findings and a hemizygous pathogenic variant in IL2RG identified by molecular genetic testing.

nodes.description: Intravenous (or subcutaneous) immunoglobulin replacement provides passive humoral protection while patients await or recover from definitive immune reconstitution.

evidence.snippet: use of immunoglobulin infusions and antibiotics, particularly prophylaxis against Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia

nodes.description: Antimicrobial prophylaxis, particularly against Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia, is a standard supportive measure to prevent opportunistic infection pending immune reconstitution.

evidence.snippet: particularly prophylaxis against Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (formerly Pneumocystis carinii) and fungal infections

nodes.description: Recurrent and opportunistic pneumonia, including Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia, is common.

evidence.snippet: Untreated ADA-SCID presents as life-threatening opportunistic illnesses in the first weeks to months of life with poor linear growth and weight gain secondary to persistent diarrhea, extensive dermatitis, and recurrent pneumonia.
Deficiency of either RAG1 or RAG2 causes T-B-NK + SCID and is inherited in an autosomal recessive pattern.

nodes.description: Persistent fungal infection, especially mucocutaneous candidiasis, reflects loss of T-cell immunity.

evidence.snippet: Clinical manifestations are characterised by recurrent and severe bacterial, viral, and fungal opportunistic infections that start from early infancy period.

nodes.description: Recurrent, severe, and opportunistic bacterial, viral, fungal, and protozoal infections beginning in early infancy.

evidence.snippet: Clinical manifestations are characterised by recurrent and severe bacterial, viral, and fungal opportunistic infections that start from early infancy period.

nodes.description: NK cells are absent in T-B+NK- forms (IL2RG, JAK3) and in T-B-NK- ADA deficiency.
nodes.id: Reduced total natural killer cell count

evidence.snippet: Characteristic immune abnormalities are lymphocytopenia (low numbers of T, B, and NK cells)

nodes.description: Profound combined defect of cellular and humoral adaptive immunity is the defining feature.

evidence.reference_title: The diagnosis of severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID): The Primary Immune Deficiency Treatment Consortium (PIDTC) 2022 Definitions.
evidence.snippet: Severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID) results from defects in the differentiation of hematopoietic stem cells into mature T lymphocytes, with additional lymphoid lineages affected in particular genotypes.

nodes.description: In ADA deficiency, loss of adenosine deaminase activity causes accumulation of adenosine and especially deoxyadenosine (and intracellular dATP), which are lymphotoxic and impair lymphocyte development, viability, and function. The metabolic toxicity affects all lymphoid lineages, producing a T-B-NK- immunophenotype.

evidence.snippet: Adenosine deaminase (ADA) deficiency is a systemic purine metabolic disorder that primarily affects lymphocyte development, viability, and function.
